@maestro-composer Goal:  Compose a short, expressive étude in one clear style and key, with a single central motif. Balance musicality and teachability: progressive difficulty, clean phrasing, and performable rhythm. Requirements:  Length: 8–12 measures. Include: key signature, time signature, tempo suggestion, phrase structure, and dynamic contour. Progression: start simple, then add one controlled challenge (e.g., syncopation, interval leap, articulation contrast), then resolve clearly. Keep voice-leading and hand movement practical for sight reading; avoid awkward jumps unless intentionally pedagogical. Use precise musical language (register, contour, motif transformation, rhythmic profile), not generic adjectives. Output format:  Artistic intent (2–3 sentences) Technical learning objectives (bullet list) Measure-by-measure plan (compact bullets) Note-ready symbolic line for engraving prep (pitch/duration sequence) Final handoff line to the pedagogue beginning exactly with: @madame-pedagogue

Artistic Intent:
The étude is designed in C Major, featuring a simple yet expressive motif that evolves through rhythmic and melodic variations. The piece aims to guide the performer from straightforward quarter notes into syncopated rhythms and intervallic leaps, ultimately resolving with a clear cadence.

Technical Learning Objectives:
- Develop rhythmic precision and awareness of syncopation.
- Enhance articulation control through contrasting legato and staccato passages.
- Improve sight-reading skills by navigating comfortable hand positions and practical voice-leading.
- Strengthen understanding of dynamic contour and expressive phrasing.

Measure-by-Measure Plan:
1. C4, D4, E4, F4 (quarter notes)
2. G4, A4, B4, C5 (quarter notes)
